feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: What else could Xenoverse 3 even add?

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Why do gamers care so much that a game feels like a "real RPG"?
entry.content: I just don't get why it's such a big deal if a game feels like a real RPG or not. I myself have never actually sat down and played a tabletop game, but I have watched examples of them, and it seems like its just a bunch of people sitting around, doing improv and playing pretend, like a play or something. Sure I guess this involves a lot of "choice" but to me it seems less like a game and more like a drama exercise or something. I don't get why gamers are just so hung up on RPGs actually being "r

entry.title: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says: Remigration is the compromise
entry.content: When I close my eyes I have visions of armed white men leading brown men at gunpoint to the beaches of Europe, there they're given a simple command: swim. The Abduls and Ahmeds will look around at each other in disbelief, they will complain and protest until a single shot cracks overhead. With trepidation they approach the waterline, grey sky overhead, the cold waves licking at their feet. They don't know how to swim, another warning shot and they are neck deep in the water. Then the firing begi

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Jai is the no-coder language
entry.content: From today onwards I officially became a Blow hater. I sent an email to Theckla asking to join Jai's closed beta. I explained that I work with networking in a Telecom company and said that I would like to study the language a little and do a demo at my company. I currently have the pirated compiler, but I can't present a pirated compiler in my company, and of course I didn't mentioned that on the email. I explained some of our constraints and also talked about some features of the language that

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Making an AI / Macro to create images for me
entry.content: /g/, I want to know how easy it is to have my PC automatically perform some basic inputs Simply put, I make money creating images on NovelAI, but it is annoying manually clicking the generate button and changing the prompt. I use cookie cutter prompts I merely change depending on what part of the image set I am on. So. How hard would it be to set up a macro or some sort of AI agent that automatically does this for me? Click generate, generate again, change prompt, generate, stop after 100x image

It feels like an actual fucking psyop, you look up who the majority of listeners are and who go to rap festivals like rolling loud or Summer Smash, it's all wealthy white people? What gives?

entry.title: /g/ Technology: After spending a few months in Shenzhen, I don't understand why the West is fall…
entry.content: After spending a few months in Shenzhen, I don't understand why the West is falling so far behind China's tech ecosystem. In Shenzhen, I can order anything and have it delivered by drone to a dropoff point pretty much immediately. Why isn't this available in US cities? It makes no sense to me. There's so much fucking money lying on the ground for anyone who just copies Chinese tech in the West.

entry.title: /trash/ Off-Topic: /trash/ Pokemon Drawthread
entry.content: Tengatink Edition Pokemon OCs and Fakemons (actual fakemons please like fusions and clovermons) are welcome too >How do I request? •Start your post with "requesting" or "/r/". •Provide descriptions, links, and image references in a single post. •Don't spam or bump your requests. •Check the dropbox or booru to see if your request was done. •Request edits in the thread for edits and colorings: >>>/trash/lineart >What if I want to start drawing? •Check these resources for beginners:https://pastebin

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Remember that VeraCrypt may fail to boot into Windows after (during?) this month…
entry.content: Remember that VeraCrypt may fail to boot into Windows after (during?) this month June 2026 because of MS signing issue. If you're running Windows encrypted with VeraCrypt it's a good idea to read through this thread to see if you need to update anything. And backup your stuff of course, just in case. https://sourceforge.net/p/veracrypt /discussion/general/thread/9620d7a4 b3/ I have not seen much news coverage about this and had almost forgotten about it myself, so consider this a friendly remind

entry.title: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says: Spikedog Cafe ®
entry.content: With EsforEsquire's recent article about the Spikedog Cafe we have gotten a lot of emails and phone calls. Many were concerned that we were selling spikedogs as food. We are not. Our owner who is short and white with an interesting hairdo would not approve. Other people have asked if we have a children's menu or meals that are the appropriate size for bearies. The answer is of course!!!

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Iron Lung... just want to make sure I got this right (I was half watching)
entry.content: >humanity is on the verge of extinction >for some reason there's a solution on some far away moon >for some other reason the moon is filled with oceans of blood >they send a death row convict to get the macguffin >turns out there's a giant psychic angler fish there who east spaceship boats >fish-monster incapacitates people by confusing them with voices > our protagonist gives his last breath getting the black box to the surface like most creepypasta inspired stuff, they take the edgy stuff too

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Was the GameCube considered a failure?
entry.content: I was listening to an old episode of GameSpot's Hot Spot podcast from 10/26/2005. They were reading an article about Nintendo basically admitting that the GameCube massively underperformed. The tone was weird, you had Jeff Gerstmann laughing at the host for going all in on the GameCube at the start of that generation. They were suggesting that Nintendo might pivot to being a game only publisher on consoles. Was it the success of the DS and the Wii that sort of smoothed over the money side of the

entry.title: /trash/ Off-Topic: /osc/ Object Show Community General #70
entry.content: Clockcest Edition! Previous: >>83177992 BFDIE Full Season: https://youtu.be/2andF8GEmC8 > What are Object Shows? Object Shows are web animations unfortunately lacking in completion with characters based on everyday items. They often compete in challenges with humor, drama, and quirky personalities. More: https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki. php/Main/ObjectShows > Notable Shows: >Battle for Dream Island The one that started it all! A competition between inanimate, anthropomorphic objects, for a gr

entry.title: /trash/ Off-Topic: /flg/ - F-List General #8376 Gadget Head Edition Previous Thread: >>83410031 F-l…
entry.content: /flg/ - F-List General #8376 Gadget Head Edition Previous Thread: >>83410031 F-list is a website for people interested in erotic roleplaying, and creating character profiles to find like-minded partners with similar interests and fetishes to roleplay with. It features a webchat (which can be used for textual roleplaying), group forums and more kinks and depravity than you can memorize. The purpose of this thread is to serve as a place where roleplayers can freely share and advertise their profil

entry.title: /int/ International: >I just learned that French not only don't have a separate word for husband and …
entry.content: >I just learned that French not only don't have a separate word for husband and wife (they just say "my man/woman"), but they also don't have a separate word for relatives, but just use "parent" for male relative, and "parente" for female relative Why are they like this? Isn't it good to be precise instead of muddling everything? French seems to just reuse words instead of coming up with new ones.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: The Slow Cancellation of the Future: Why Mark Fisher Believed Music Innovation Stalled
entry.content: If you look at the landscape of 21st-century music, a strange paradox emerges. We have more access to the entirety of recorded human history than ever before, yet our current sonic era feels haunted by the past. In his seminal 2014 book Ghosts of My Life, cultural theorist Mark Fisher articulated a haunting thesis: music innovation has effectively ceased. Borrowing a phrase from Italian philosopher Franco "Bifo" Berardi, Fisher called this phenomenon "the slow cancellation of the future." Here i

entry.title: /g/ Technology: I've been having this problem all my life, my family doesn't seem to understand …
entry.content: I've been having this problem all my life, my family doesn't seem to understand how electricity works. They don't understand that rechargeable batteries put out a lower voltage than regular batteries, meaning that they won't work for many things such as digital cameras, etc. They also don't seem to understand that you can't just leave them on the charger, you have to take them off when they are full, otherwise the battery is ruined and unsuitable for use in even a television remote. Finally, the
